encounter.reason: HFrEF and paroxysmal atrial fibrillation follow-up

conditions.name: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction, NYHA class II to III

medications.name: carvedilol 6.25 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 6.25 mg by mouth twice daily
medications.reason: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction

medications.name: furosemide 20 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 20 mg by mouth every morning
medications.reason: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction / volume management

medications.name: losartan oral therapy
medications.reason: replaced by sacubitril/valsartan for guideline-directed therapy in symptomatic HFrEF

medications.name: sacubitril/valsartan 24/26 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: start 24/26 mg by mouth twice daily after appropriate washout
medications.reason: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction

medications.name: apixaban 5 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 5 mg by mouth twice daily
medications.reason: stroke prevention in paroxysmal atrial fibrillation

document_references.name: Progress note
document_references.description: Cardiology follow-up note documenting symptomatic HFrEF with exertional dyspnea and paroxysmal atrial fibrillation with intermittent palpitations. The note should summarize reviewed echocardiogram, ECG, medication changes including losartan discontinuation, sacubitril/valsartan initiation, apixaban initiation, and follow-up planning.

document_references.name: Patient instructions
document_references.description: Counseling document covering sodium restriction, fluid restriction, symptom monitoring, and return precautions for worsening dyspnea, edema, bleeding, or sustained palpitations. It should also reinforce medication changes and planned reassessment in 3 to 4 weeks.

document_references.name: ECG interpretation note
document_references.description: Separate interpretation documenting same-day ECG findings of sinus rhythm at 72 bpm with premature atrial complexes, contrasted with irregular intake rhythm and prior atrial fibrillation history. The note should make clear that no acute ischemic symptoms were reported.

diagnostic_reports.description: echocardiogram was reviewed at this visit and showed left ventricular ejection fraction 35%, mild left ventricular dilation, global hypokinesis, grade II diastolic dysfunction, and mild left atrial enlargement. This encounter narrative does not provide a full structured echocardiography result set beyond the summarized findings.
same-day ECG interpretation documented sinus rhythm at 72 bpm with premature atrial complexes. Narrative provides interpretive findings rather than a full waveform-derived structured result set.

diagnostic_reports.type: Ambulatory rhythm monitor report from earlier 2022
diagnostic_reports.description: prior report was reviewed during this encounter and had captured brief atrial fibrillation runs up to 140 bpm. This was referenced to support assessment of paroxysmal atrial fibrillation and anticoagulation planning; it was not newly resulted at this visit.
